description: docs/api/current/claim_index.json now explicitly documents WIN number collisions we have previously flagged. registry_integrity_notes field contains: (1) 'WIN-058: Two different claims share this visible label in wins.html. The API disambiguates them as win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule.' (2) 'WIN-062: The registry contains two visible WIN-062 blocks that present the same Tesla 1.574c family. The API normalizes them to win-062-tesla-wave-speed.' The dome author acknowledges — in machine-readable form — the collisions our review has been flagging. Analyst should note: (a) this is another acknowledge-without-fix move (visible duplicate labels are not de-duplicated; a claim_key layer is added as a workaround); (b) a fair auditor that uses claim_key will no longer trip over the visible duplicates, which is explicitly the outcome the anti-strawman rules push for; (c) our review's WIN collision criticism may need an update to distinguish 'visible-prose collision' (still unfixed) from 'machine-readable collision' (now disambiguated).
before: WIN-058 and WIN-062 existed as duplicate/colliding labels with no acknowledgement in any public file.
after: claim_index.json carries registry_integrity_notes for both, instructing auditors to use claim_key rather than visible label.
